nodes.name: fb_ads_library.py
nodes.summary: Wraps the Facebook Ads Library API to search competitor ads (by keyword or page id), resolve Facebook page URLs to page ids, and format ad results for downstream LLM analysis.

nodes.name: search_competitor_ads
nodes.summary: Searches the Facebook Ads Library for competitor ads matching a search term and country/region filters.

nodes.name: search_by_page_id
nodes.summary: Searches the Facebook Ads Library for all active ads run by a specific page id.

nodes.name: format_ads_for_analysis
nodes.summary: Formats raw Ads Library API results into a structured text block suitable for LLM-based competitor analysis.

nodes.name: is_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether the Facebook Ads Library integration is configured and usable.

nodes.name: _extract_fb_candidate
nodes.summary: Extracts a candidate Facebook page identifier or URL fragment from arbitrary user-provided text.

nodes.name: resolve_fb_url
nodes.summary: Resolves a Facebook URL or vanity name to a canonical page id by querying the Graph API or Ads Library.

nodes.name: resolve_page_id_from_url
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that extracts a Facebook candidate from text and resolves it to a page id.

nodes.name: fb_marketing.py
nodes.summary: Wraps the Facebook Marketing API to fetch account/ad-level insights, manage campaigns and ad sets (status changes, budget updates), and format performance data for LLM-based analysis and the ads optimizer.

nodes.name: get_account_insights
nodes.summary: Fetches aggregate ad account performance insights (spend, ROAS, CPA, etc.) from the Facebook Marketing API over a date range.

nodes.name: get_account_insights_daily
nodes.summary: Fetches day-by-day breakdown of ad account performance insights from the Facebook Marketing API.

nodes.name: get_active_campaigns
nodes.summary: Fetches the list of currently active campaigns for an ad account from the Facebook Marketing API.

nodes.name: _extract_action_value
nodes.summary: Extracts a specific action's metric value (e.g. purchases) from a Facebook Insights actions array.

nodes.name: _hook_hold_rates
nodes.summary: Computes video hook and hold retention rates from Facebook video metrics.

nodes.name: format_insights_for_analysis
nodes.summary: Formats account-level insights data into a structured text block for LLM-based performance analysis.

nodes.name: format_ad_level_for_analysis
nodes.summary: Formats ad-level performance data (including hook/hold rates) into a structured text block for LLM-based ad analysis.

nodes.name: is_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether the Facebook Marketing API integration is configured and usable.

nodes.name: has_token
nodes.summary: Checks whether a valid Facebook access token is available for API calls.

nodes.name: get_campaigns_with_adsets
nodes.summary: Fetches campaigns along with their nested ad sets from the Facebook Marketing API for the ads optimizer hierarchy view.

nodes.name: get_ads_by_adset
nodes.summary: Fetches all ads belonging to a specific ad set from the Facebook Marketing API.

nodes.name: set_object_status
nodes.summary: Updates the active/paused status of a Facebook campaign, ad set, or ad object via the Marketing API.

nodes.name: update_budget
nodes.summary: Updates the daily or lifetime budget of a Facebook campaign or ad set via the Marketing API.

nodes.name: format_hierarchy_for_optimizer
nodes.summary: Formats the campaign/ad-set/ad hierarchy with performance metrics into a structured text block for the LLM-driven ads optimizer.

nodes.name: image_gen.py
nodes.summary: Wraps an AI image generation/editing provider to generate marketing images, edit existing images, analyze image style, and estimate generation cost.

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the client used for image generation API calls.

nodes.name: generate_image
nodes.summary: Generates a new marketing image from a text prompt using the configured AI image generation provider.

nodes.name: edit_image
nodes.summary: Edits an existing image according to a text instruction using the AI image editing provider.

nodes.name: analyze_image_style
nodes.summary: Analyzes an uploaded image's visual style attributes via the AI vision provider for use in style-matched generation.

nodes.name: estimate_cost
nodes.summary: Estimates the monetary cost of an image generation or editing request based on provider pricing.

nodes.name: is_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether the image generation integration is configured and usable.

nodes.name: llm_router.py
nodes.summary: Multi-provider LLM router abstracting Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, and Perplexity behind a unified call() interface with task-type-based provider selection, fallback chains, and availability reporting.

nodes.name: Provider
nodes.summary: Enum of supported LLM providers (Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, Perplexity) used to select which backend handles a given call.

nodes.name: TaskType
nodes.summary: Enum of task categories (e.g. reasoning, fast-extraction, grounded-search) used to map requests to the most appropriate provider/model.

nodes.name: ProviderUnavailable
nodes.summary: Exception raised when a specific LLM provider is not configured or unreachable for a requested call.

nodes.name: AllProvidersFailedError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when every provider in the fallback chain fails to fulfill a call.

nodes.name: _get_anthropic_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the Anthropic client instance.

nodes.name: _call_anthropic_sonnet
nodes.summary: Invokes the Anthropic Claude Sonnet model with the given prompt and parameters.

nodes.name: _call_anthropic_haiku
nodes.summary: Invokes the Anthropic Claude Haiku model with the given prompt and parameters, used for fast/cheap extraction tasks.

nodes.name: _get_gemini_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the Google Gemini client instance.

nodes.name: _calc_thinking_budget
nodes.summary: Computes the reasoning/thinking token budget to allocate for a Gemini call based on task complexity.

nodes.name: _call_gemini_pro
nodes.summary: Invokes the Gemini Pro model with the given prompt, handling thinking budget and response parsing.

nodes.name: _patch_homepage_links
nodes.summary: Post-processes Gemini grounded-search responses to fix or inject homepage links into the generated content.

nodes.name: _call_gemini_pro_grounded
nodes.summary: Invokes the Gemini Pro model with web-grounded search enabled for tasks requiring up-to-date factual grounding.

nodes.name: _call_gemini_flash
nodes.summary: Invokes the faster, cheaper Gemini Flash model for lighter-weight tasks.

nodes.name: _get_openai_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the OpenAI client instance.

nodes.name: _call_openai_generic
nodes.summary: Generic helper that invokes a specified OpenAI model with the given prompt and parameters, shared by the model-specific wrapper functions.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt5
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-5 model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt5_mini
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-5 Mini model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt5_nano
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-5 Nano model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt_4_1_mini
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-4.1 Mini model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt4o
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-4o model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt4o_mini
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-4o Mini model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_perplexity_sonar
nodes.summary: Invokes the Perplexity Sonar model, typically used for web-grounded research queries.

nodes.name: call
nodes.summary: Main entrypoint of the LLM router: selects a provider chain based on TaskType, attempts each provider in order with fallback, and raises AllProvidersFailedError if all fail.

nodes.name: _forced_test_provider
nodes.summary: Allows tests/debugging to force the router to use a specific provider, bypassing normal selection logic.

nodes.name: is_provider_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether a given provider's API key/credentials are configured and usable.

nodes.name: availability_report
nodes.summary: Builds a summary report of which LLM providers are currently available/configured.

nodes.name: video_vision.py
nodes.summary: Extracts keyframes from uploaded videos via ffmpeg probing/extraction and analyzes them with an AI vision model to produce a visual analysis summary for marketing content review.

nodes.name: is_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether the video vision analysis integration is configured and usable.

nodes.name: availability_report
nodes.summary: Builds a summary report describing the availability status of the video vision integration.

nodes.name: extract_keyframes
nodes.summary: Extracts a set of representative keyframes from a video file at selected timestamps using ffmpeg.

nodes.name: _choose_timestamps
nodes.summary: Chooses a set of evenly-spaced or heuristically-significant timestamps within a video's duration for keyframe extraction.

nodes.name: _probe_duration
nodes.summary: Probes a video file's total duration using ffprobe.

nodes.name: _extract_one_frame
nodes.summary: Extracts a single frame from a video at a specified timestamp using ffmpeg.

nodes.name: analyze_frames_with_vision
nodes.summary: Sends extracted video keyframes to an AI vision model for analysis (e.g. content quality, hook strength).

nodes.name: extract_visual_analysis
nodes.summary: High-level orchestration function that extracts keyframes from a video and runs vision analysis on them, returning a combined visual analysis result.

nodes.name: feedback_digest.py
nodes.summary: Background worker that formats and runs a periodic digest job summarizing recent user feedback for review.

nodes.name: format_digest
nodes.summary: Formats aggregated feedback digest data into a readable summary report.

nodes.name: run_once
nodes.summary: Runs a single pass of the feedback digest worker: gathers digest data, formats it, and delivers/logs the result.

nodes.name: monitor_competitors.py
nodes.summary: Background worker that periodically checks tracked competitors' Facebook ad activity via the Ads Library and notifies the bot/user of new ads detected.

nodes.name: _ad_ids_from_response
nodes.summary: Extracts the set of ad ids present in a Facebook Ads Library API response.

nodes.name: check_one
nodes.summary: Checks a single tracked competitor for new ad activity since the last check, comparing current ad ids to the previously stored set.

nodes.name: run_once_with_bot
nodes.summary: Runs a single monitoring pass across all due tracked competitors and sends Telegram notifications via the bot for any new ads found.

nodes.name: start_background_monitor
nodes.summary: Starts a recurring background task that periodically triggers the competitor monitoring check.

nodes.name: run_once
nodes.summary: Runs a single monitoring pass across all due tracked competitors without requiring a bot instance, primarily for manual/CLI invocation.
